one_line_thesis: Autodesk is a global design-and-engineering software compounder with durable subscription revenue and strong free cash flow ($11.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Autodesk is a global design-and-engineering software compounder with durable subscription revenue and strong free cash flow ($11.20 per share in FY2026). At ~$218, filing-based owner cash implies 15.7% per year over 7 years. Moat is stable; partial dhando because stock-based compensation and competition cap downside. Marvin proposes watch; [HUMAN REVIEW] SBC normalization and Starboard margin thesis.

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2026 10-K: stockholders' equity; economic value in normalized owner cash ($11.2047/sh), not GAAP book alone.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of ADSK, including subscription owner cash, construction cloud/AI upside, net financial claims, and transition/competition reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Consolidated subscription engine valued through owner-cash discount on FY2026 FCF per share; cloud/AI option, net liquidity, and reserve are separate overlap keys.

timeline.label: Revenue moved +17.5% versus the prior period (6,131.0 to 7,206.0).
Operating Cash Flow moved +52.6% versus the prior period (1,607.0 to 2,452.0).
Q1 FY2027: revenue $1.84B; net income $491M; EPS $2.32 diluted; OCF $893M — steady subscription cadence
FY2026 results: revenue $6.74B (+18% YoY); OCF $2.45B; FCF ~$2.41B; RPO $8.3B; $1.4B buybacks
Board and executive compensation framework updated for FY2027 proxy cycle (DEF 14A filed May 2026)
Starboard Value filed activist proxy materials (DFAN14A) seeking board engagement on capital allocation and margins
Starboard Value initial DFAN14A filing — campaign active on governance and operating efficiency
FY2025 results: revenue $5.72B; subscription transition nearing completion; operating margin recovery underway
Construction cloud and Forma platform investments accelerate; data-centric AEC workflow pivot
Named-user subscription model rollout largely complete; perpetual license phase-out for most products
Soroban Capital disclosed passive 13G position (context tier; no active campaign)
Shift to recurring subscription revenue model announced; multi-year transition from perpetual licenses

industry.structure: Design and engineering software oligopoly: Autodesk, Dassault Systèmes, Bentley Systems, and Trimble compete on workflow integration, switching costs, and industry-specific tools. High recurring revenue and strong customer lock-in on file formats and training.
industry.share_shift: Autodesk maintains leadership in AEC (Revit, AutoCAD) and manufacturing (Fusion); cloud-native challengers and open-source tools gain on price in SMB segments. Construction cloud and data platform investments aim to widen the moat.
industry.trend: AI-assisted design, generative modeling, and cloud collaboration are reshaping workflows. Subscription transition is largely complete; growth now driven by ARPU upsell, construction cloud, and international expansion.

top_claims.claim: EPS (basic) growth moved from +25.8% to +228.2% YoY in the latest quarter. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.
Growth regime strengthening: YoY growth moved to +12.2% to +228.2% YoY (baseline median +22.8% YoY) in the latest quarter. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

Autodesk is a global design-and-engineering software compounder with durable subscription revenue and strong free cash flow ($11.20 per share in FY2026). At ~$218, filing-based owner cash implies 15.7% per year over 7 years. Moat is stable; partial dhando because stock-based compensation and competition cap downside. M

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Stock-based compensation and competition compress free cash flow per share faster than revenue growth, leaving shareholders with modest returns despite a quality franchise.
Construction cycle: AEC revenue tied to building starts; deep recession would slow billings and RPO growth.
Open-source / low-cost rivals: FreeCAD and cloud-native tools attack SMB and education segments.
